behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: This is a data-preparation logic bug—the failure mode is that the system sends the full file instead of just changed lines to the analyzer. This is pure logic that can be caught with mocked GitHub API responses if the mock accurately represents diff structure. Edge cases are minimal; Unit tests with good diff fixtures are sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the GitHub API to return a diff with M total lines and N changed lines. Invoke the input-scoping logic and verify that only the N changed lines (with correct line numbers) are passed to the downstream analyzer, not the full M lines.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test focuses on logic/filtering, which is Unit-level. A mock API is sufficient because the failure is about correctness of scoping logic, not about matching real GitHub behavior.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Line-position mapping to GitHub's diff API is subtle and error-prone (diff position vs file line, handling renames/deletes). A Unit test with a mocked GitHub API could hide off-by-one errors or format mismatches if the mock doesn't precisely match GitHub's behavior. Integration tests with real diff structures or validated fixtures that closely match GitHub's actual format are needed to catch edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Given a pull request diff with code changes at multiple line ranges, post inline comments through the system and verify that when viewed in GitHub's diff interface, comments appear at the exact lines where the findings occur, with correct position indexing.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires realistic diff structures to validate line-position mapping. Integration-level testing with fixtures or real diffs catches subtle bugs that mocks might hide.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly tests whether the actual LLM/analyzer identifies a real bug. Mocking the analyzer defeats the test purpose—the whole point is to validate the real model's defect-detection capability. The outcome is non-deterministic; different runs may vary in what the LLM detects. Only invoking the real analyzer on real code with a known defect can catch this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the code review analyzer on a pull request containing a known, detectable defect (e.g., missing null check, unhandled exception, logic error). Verify that the analyzer flags at least one finding related to that defect in its output.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test must use the real LLM/analyzer and real code. Mocking would not test the intended behavior.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: The failure is data-consistency logic: 'summary includes findings without inline comments.' A Unit test with mocked analyzer output could catch basic logic bugs. However, real failures often emerge when the actual analyzer produces edge cases (duplicate findings, conflicting severity levels) that the summary/comment-posting logic handles inconsistently. Integration testing with real analyzer output (mocked GitHub for determinism) is safer and catches realistic failure scenarios.
behaviors.test_description: Verify that after the analyzer produces a complete set of findings, every finding included in the top-level PR summary comment has a corresponding inline comment posted at the relevant line in the diff. Confirm no findings appear in the summary without matching inline comments.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Testing with real analyzer output ensures we catch edge cases that mocks might miss, such as findings with unusual classifications or duplicates.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly tests the complete end-to-end pipeline with multiple agentic and system steps in sequence: fetch diff → analyze code (LLM) → post inline comments (GitHub API) → post summary (GitHub API). Failure modes include partial sequence breakdown, API errors, timeouts, state inconsistency between steps, and orchestration failures. Multi-step workflows with real LLM and real GitHub API calls can only be validated at Workflow level.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the complete code review workflow on a real or realistic test pull request: fetch and parse the PR diff, invoke the analyzer to identify defects, post inline review comments for each finding, and post a top-level summary comment. Verify all steps complete successfully without manual intervention, with no hangs or failures, and that the final PR state (inline comments + summary) is correct and consistent.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test must orchestrate multiple agentic and system steps end-to-end. Unit/Integration tests of individual steps cannot catch multi-step orchestration failures or timing issues.
